summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on enhancing the `Contents` view in `@plone/contents` to improve usability and accessibility. The goal is to refactor the contents view, making it a mini-app that operates independently from the site frame, improving overall user experience. 

## What needs to be done
- Refactor the `Contents.jsx` component to utilize the `@plone/contents` core package.
- Implement better accessibility features using `react-aria-components`.
- Avoid using SemanticUI and instead adopt TypeScript and better component slicing for maintainability.
- Update related issues to ensure all improvements are integrated effectively.

## Helpful background
This issue is part of the Plone Improvement Proposal (PLIP) process and aims to provide a better content management experience within the Plone ecosystem. There have been discussions around scheduling this for Plone 6.2, indicating a move away from implementing it in the earlier version.

## Good first steps
1. Familiarize yourself with the current code of `Contents.jsx`.
2. Review related issues to understand the scope of enhancements.
3. Check the implementation patterns in the `@plone/contents` package.
4. Join discussions on the issue thread for clarification and coordination with other contributors. 

Testing and documentation updates will be expected after implementation. Happy contributing!
